title: China-to-Ecuador Solar PV Inverter Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: EMC — IEC 62920 and IEC 61000 Series for PV Inverters — INEN Conformity and Ecuador Grid Requirements
rows.target_requirement: Ecuador's INEN conformity framework for PV inverters adopts IEC-based EMC standards. The primary EMC standard for PV power conversion equipment is IEC 62920 (Photovoltaic power generating systems — EMC requirements and test methods for power conversion equipment). IEC 61000-3-2 (Electromagnetic compatibility — Limits for harmonic current emissions for equipment with input current up to 16 A per phase) and the broader IEC 61000 series apply to harmonic emission and immunity requirements. For INEN conformity certification, EMC evidence must be based on the applicable IEC standards — not China domestic EMC standards. Because Ecuador's grid operates at 60 Hz (not 50 Hz), EMC and harmonic tests must be conducted at 60 Hz to be valid for Ecuador grid conditions; results obtained at 50 Hz under Chinese grid conditions may not be directly transferable. ARCERNNR and distribution companies may require EMC evidence as part of grid-connection technical documentation for microgeneración projects.
target_standards: IEC 62920 — Photovoltaic power generating systems — EMC requirements and test methods for power conversion equipment (adopted as INEN conformity basis)
IEC 61000-3-2 — Electromagnetic compatibility — Limits for harmonic current emissions (equipment with input current up to 16 A per phase)
IEC 61000 series — Electromagnetic compatibility — broader immunity and emission requirements
NTE INEN / RTE INEN — Ecuador technical standards and regulations adopting IEC EMC requirements for PV inverters
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese PV inverter EMC is assessed under GB/T 37408-2021 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters), which includes EMC test requirements aligned with China's domestic framework at 50 Hz / 220/380 V. GB/T 37408 EMC testing is conducted at CNAS-accredited Chinese laboratories. While IEC 62920 draws on IEC 61000-series test methods, the GB/T 37408 EMC test scope and referenced limits are not harmonised with IEC 62920 and IEC 61000-3-2 as required by INEN Ecuador conformity. Chinese EMC certificates based solely on GB/T 37408 are not accepted as IEC 62920 or IEC 61000-3-2 compliance evidence for INEN conformity certification or ARCERNNR project submissions. Furthermore, 50 Hz-based EMC test results are not directly valid for Ecuador's 60 Hz grid.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters) — includes 50 Hz-based EMC test requirements
rows.gap: Chinese GB/T 37408 EMC testing does not satisfy INEN's IEC 62920 and IEC 61000-3-2 requirements for Ecuador conformity certification. The 60 Hz frequency difference is an additional gap: EMC and harmonic test results obtained at 50 Hz under Chinese grid conditions may not be valid for Ecuador's 60 Hz grid, and retesting at 60 Hz may be required by INEN or the ARCERNNR project engineer. Exporters should obtain: (a) IEC 62920 EMC type-test report from an ILAC-accredited laboratory, conducted at 60 Hz and covering the specific inverter model at its Ecuador-configuration power rating; (b) IEC 61000-3-2 harmonic current emission test report at 60 Hz for the model. Confirm with the INEN-accredited conformity body whether 60 Hz retesting is required relative to any existing 50 Hz IEC 62920 test reports held for other markets.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for INEN conformity certification (market entry gate); IEC 62920 and IEC 61000-3-2 evidence at 60 Hz are the required basis; ARCERNNR and CNEL may require EMC evidence as part of microgeneración grid-connection documentation
mandatory_status.cn: Voluntary — GB/T 37408 EMC testing is a requirement for voluntary CQC/CGC certification; not a standalone mandatory market-access requirement for domestic China PV inverters

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Chinese GB/T 37408 EMC certificates do not satisfy INEN's IEC 62920 and IEC 61000-3-2 requirements for Ecuador conformity certification. Obtain IEC 62920 and IEC 61000-3-2 type-test reports from an ILAC-accredited laboratory for the specific inverter model at its 60 Hz Ecuador configuration before submitting for INEN conformity. Confirm with the INEN conformity body whether EMC test results at 50 Hz (from testing for other markets) are accepted or whether 60 Hz retesting is required. Verify current INEN technical regulations for PV inverters at normalizacion.gob.ec.

rows.requirement: Ecuador Grid Parameters — 60 Hz / 120/240 V Split-Phase (LV) and ARCERNNR / CNEL Grid-Connection Requirements
rows.target_requirement: Ecuador's distribution grid operates at 60 Hz and 120/240 V split-phase at low voltage — a fundamentally different electrical environment from China's 50 Hz / 220/380 V grid. This is a genuine engineering gap, not a documentation gap. Key requirements include: (1) inverter firmware must be reconfigured for 60 Hz operation — grid-protection relay settings, anti-islanding detection windows, reconnection delays, and harmonic thresholds are all frequency-dependent and must be validated for 60 Hz; (2) voltage settings must be reconfigured for 120/240 V split-phase LV — not 220/380 V; (3) IEC 62116 anti-islanding compliance must be validated at 60 Hz and at Ecuador's voltage levels; (4) ARCERNNR (Agencia de Regulación y Control de Energía y Recursos Naturales No Renovables) sets the regulatory framework for grid connection and distributed generation (microgeneración) under its resolutions and technical regulations; (5) distribution companies (CNEL EP and regional distributors) impose technical connection conditions, including protection coordination and meter requirements for net-metering (microgeneración). Inverters not configured and tested for 60 Hz / 120/240 V cannot be lawfully connected to the Ecuador grid regardless of other certifications held.
target_standards: IEC 62116 — Utility-interconnected photovoltaic inverters — Test procedure of islanding prevention measures (must be validated at 60 Hz for Ecuador)
IEC 61727 — Photovoltaic (PV) systems — Characteristics of the utility interface (60 Hz grid interface requirements)
ARCERNNR resolutions and technical regulations on microgeneración (distributed PV grid connection and net-metering)
CNEL EP and regional distribution-company connection technical conditions
Ecuador LV grid: 60 Hz, 120/240 V split-phase
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese grid-connection evidence commonly includes GB/T 19964-2024 (Technical Requirements for Connecting Photovoltaic Power Station to Power System, applicable to utility-scale PV via 10 kV and above), NB/T 32004 (inverter technical specifications), and domestic anti-islanding test results. These Chinese grid-connection documents are based on China's 50 Hz / 220/380 V grid conditions and are entirely inapplicable to Ecuador's 60 Hz / 120/240 V split-phase grid. The frequency difference (50 Hz vs 60 Hz) means firmware anti-islanding and protection relay settings certified under Chinese conditions are not valid for Ecuador without full reconfiguration and retesting. Chinese grid-connection documents provide no presumption of compliance with ARCERNNR or CNEL connection requirements.
china_standards: GB/T 19964-2024 — 光伏发电站接入电力系统技术规定 (Technical Requirements for Connecting Photovoltaic Power Station to Power System)
GB/T 156 — 标准电压 (Standard voltages) — China grid: 220/380 V, 50 Hz
NB/T 32004-2018 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters) — 50 Hz-based
rows.gap: Gap: China's 50 Hz / 220/380 V grid-connection documents do not satisfy ARCERNNR or CNEL Ecuador grid-connection requirements. The 60 Hz / 120/240 V grid difference is a fundamental engineering reconfiguration requirement, not a paperwork gap. Exporters must: (a) reconfigure inverter firmware for 60 Hz operation and 120/240 V split-phase LV — document all parameter changes; (b) obtain IEC 62116 anti-islanding test evidence validated at 60 Hz and Ecuador voltage levels from an ILAC-accredited laboratory; (c) obtain IEC 61727 compliance evidence for the 60 Hz utility interface; (d) confirm protection relay settings and reconnection delays are validated for Ecuador's grid conditions; (e) engage an ARCERNNR-registered engineer and the relevant CNEL or distribution company for the specific microgeneración connection application. Firmware reconfiguration from 50 Hz to 60 Hz is material and may require re-type-testing under IEC 62109-2 and IEC 62116 depending on the extent of the changes.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for grid connection under ARCERNNR regulations and CNEL/distribution-company connection conditions; 60 Hz / 120/240 V reconfiguration and IEC 62116 at 60 Hz are non-negotiable engineering and regulatory gates
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese grid-connection evidence satisfies China's 50 Hz / 220/380 V grid operator requirements but does not transfer to Ecuador's 60 Hz / 120/240 V ARCERNNR/CNEL grid-connection acceptance

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Ecuador's 60 Hz / 120/240 V split-phase grid requires full inverter firmware reconfiguration and retesting — this is an engineering requirement, not a paperwork gap. Chinese 50 Hz-based grid-connection documents do not satisfy ARCERNNR or CNEL Ecuador grid-connection requirements. Reconfigure firmware for 60 Hz and 120/240 V, document all parameter changes, and obtain IEC 62116 anti-islanding test evidence at 60 Hz from an ILAC-accredited laboratory before engaging with ARCERNNR and the relevant CNEL or distribution-company connection process. Verify current ARCERNNR microgeneración regulations and CNEL connection technical conditions directly at controlrecursosyenergia.gob.ec before finalising the equipment compliance package.

source.publisher: ARCERNNR (Agencia de Regulación y Control de Energía y Recursos Naturales No Renovables)

rows.requirement: ARCERNNR Microgeneración Programme — Distributed PV Grid Connection and Net-Metering
rows.target_requirement: ARCERNNR regulates distributed grid-connected solar PV under Ecuador's microgeneración framework, which allows residential and commercial customers to install grid-tied PV systems and export surplus electricity under a net-metering or net-billing arrangement. Key requirements include: (a) installation by a qualified professional (technician or engineer registered with ARCERNNR or the relevant provincial body); (b) inverter compliance with applicable INEN technical standards and IEC standards for safety and anti-islanding; (c) a formal connection application to the relevant distribution company (CNEL EP or regional distributor) including technical documentation of the inverter and installation; (d) bidirectional meter installation by the distribution company; (e) technical inspection and approval before energisation. Chinese PV inverters supplied to Ecuador microgeneración projects must satisfy INEN conformity requirements and the distribution company's technical connection conditions, including operation at 60 Hz / 120/240 V.
target_standards: ARCERNNR microgeneración resolutions and technical regulations (distributed solar grid connection and net-metering framework)
CNEL EP and regional distribution-company technical connection conditions for microgeneración
NTE INEN technical standards applicable to PV inverters (INEN conformity required for market entry)
IEC 62116 — Anti-islanding test procedure (must be validated at 60 Hz for Ecuador)
IEC 61727 — Photovoltaic (PV) systems — Characteristics of the utility interface
rows.china_equivalent: China does not have a direct equivalent programme. Distributed solar connection in China is governed by National Energy Administration (NEA) distributed PV policies and grid operator (State Grid, Southern Power Grid) connection rules, which reference China's domestic standards (NB/T 32004, GB/T 37408, GB/T 19964-2024) and are based on 50 Hz / 220/380 V grid conditions. These Chinese distributed connection rules are not applicable in Ecuador and do not give any presumption of compliance with ARCERNNR microgeneración equipment requirements or CNEL connection conditions.
china_standards: NB/T 32004-2018 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
GB/T 19964-2024 — 光伏发电站接入电力系统技术规定 (Technical Requirements for Connecting Photovoltaic Power Station to Power System)
rows.gap: Gap: Chinese inverters for Ecuador microgeneración projects must be configured for 60 Hz / 120/240 V operation, satisfy INEN conformity requirements, and pass the relevant CNEL or distribution-company technical review as part of the microgeneración connection application. Chinese distributed solar connection approvals (NEA policy, State Grid/Southern Power Grid rules) do not transfer to Ecuador ARCERNNR microgeneración requirements. Confirm inverter 60 Hz / 120/240 V reconfiguration, INEN conformity certificate, IEC safety and anti-islanding evidence, and registration of the installing technician or engineer with the relevant CNEL or distribution company before committing product to Ecuador microgeneración projects.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for all distributed solar grid-connection projects under ARCERNNR's microgeneración framework; 60 Hz / 120/240 V inverter configuration and CNEL/distribution-company technical review are non-negotiable project gates
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese distributed solar connection approvals are granted under China NEA policy and grid operator rules at 50 Hz / 220/380 V — not transferable to Ecuador

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] ARCERNNR microgeneración is a separate project and equipment approval gate from any Chinese domestic distributed solar approval. Inverters must be reconfigured and tested for 60 Hz / 120/240 V operation before any INEN or ARCERNNR submission. Confirm inverter model eligibility, INEN conformity status, IEC safety and anti-islanding evidence, and the registration of the installing engineer with the relevant CNEL or distribution company before project submission. Verify current ARCERNNR microgeneración regulations and CNEL connection technical requirements directly at controlrecursosyenergia.gob.ec.

source.publisher: ARCERNNR (Agencia de Regulación y Control de Energía y Recursos Naturales No Renovables)

rows.requirement: IEC 62109-1 / IEC 62109-2 Safety Evidence — INEN Conformity and ARCERNNR Grid-Connection Requirements for Ecuador
rows.target_requirement: Ecuador's standards body INEN (Servicio Ecuatoriano de Normalización) issues NTE INEN technical standards and RTE INEN technical regulations that govern product conformity for market entry. For solar PV inverters, the applicable safety standards are IEC 62109-1 (Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 1: General requirements) and IEC 62109-2 (Part 2: Particular requirements for inverters), which INEN adopts as the basis for conformity assessment. A certificate of conformity to the applicable RTE INEN / NTE INEN standard is required before a PV inverter can be lawfully imported and placed on the Ecuador market. Third-party type-test evidence from an ILAC-accredited laboratory covering IEC 62109-1/-2 is the expected evidentiary basis for INEN conformity. ARCERNNR and distribution companies require this conformity evidence as part of the microgeneración connection application.
target_standards: IEC 62109-2 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 2: Particular requirements for inverters (adopted as NTE INEN basis for conformity assessment)
IEC 62109-1 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 1: General requirements (parent standard, implicitly applicable)
RTE INEN — Reglamento Técnico Ecuatoriano (Ecuador Technical Regulation) applicable to PV inverters
NTE INEN — Norma Técnica Ecuatoriana (Ecuador Technical Standard) for PV inverters
ARCERNNR microgeneración regulations requiring INEN conformity evidence for grid connection
rows.china_equivalent: China's primary voluntary standard for PV grid-connected inverter safety is GB/T 37408-2021 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters), which covers classification, environmental conditions, safety, electrical performance, EMC, and markings. GB/T 37408 is a China domestic standard assessed by CNAS-accredited Chinese laboratories, based on 50 Hz / 220/380 V grid conditions. It shares conceptual safety intent with IEC 62109-1/-2 but is not harmonised with IEC 62109 and is not accepted by INEN or ARCERNNR as equivalent to IEC 62109 type-test evidence. Chinese safety certificates based solely on GB/T 37408 are not a valid basis for INEN conformity certification for market entry into Ecuador.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Chinese GB/T 37408 safety certification does not satisfy INEN's IEC 62109-1/-2 type-test requirement for Ecuador market entry or ARCERNNR microgeneración grid-connection. Exporters must obtain IEC 62109-1/-2 type-test certificates from an ILAC-accredited laboratory covering the specific inverter model or family being supplied to Ecuador. Note that IEC 62109-2 safety testing must account for the inverter's intended operating conditions — if the inverter is reconfigured for 60 Hz / 120/240 V Ecuador operation, the safety type-test evidence should cover that configuration. Existing Chinese safety certificates based solely on GB/T 37408 must be supplemented with IEC 62109-1/-2 test reports before submitting equipment documentation for INEN conformity certification and ARCERNNR/CNEL project review.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for INEN conformity certification (market entry gate) and ARCERNNR/CNEL microgeneración grid-connection submission; IEC 62109-1/-2 type-test evidence is the required evidentiary basis
mandatory_status.cn: Voluntary — GB/T 37408-2021 is a recommended national standard (GB/T designation); CQC/CGC voluntary certification available but not required under Chinese law

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] A solar PV inverter holding only Chinese GB/T 37408 certification cannot satisfy INEN's IEC 62109-1/-2 conformity requirement for Ecuador market entry. Plan for IEC 62109-1/-2 type testing by an ILAC-accredited laboratory before submitting for INEN conformity certification. Ensure that safety type-test evidence covers the 60 Hz / 120/240 V configuration intended for Ecuador — not the 50 Hz / 220/380 V Chinese configuration. Verify current INEN technical regulations and RTE INEN scope for PV inverters directly at normalizacion.gob.ec and confirm ARCERNNR microgeneración documentation requirements at controlrecursosyenergia.gob.ec before committing to a test programme.
